2606.22510.title: Fed-CausalDiff: Decoupled Synchronization for Federated Do-Simulation and Policy Evaluation
2606.22510.paper_summary: While federated learning enables collaborative modelling on decentralised data, standard methods merely fit historical observations. This purely observational approach is fundamentally insufficient for interventional inference and policy evaluation, as sequential actions dynamically alter future states. We propose \textbf{Fed-CausalDiff}, a federated causal diffusion framework for do-simulation. The architecture decomposes the evolution of the latent state into a global causal score function and a local confounding score function. This design enables \emph{decoupled synchronisation} (DSS), where clients aggregate only the shared causal mechanism while retaining site-specific confounders locally to handle heterogeneity. Experiments on four datasets demonstrate that Fed-CausalDiff achieves better ATE and policy-value estimation accuracy, offering a favorable trade-off between communication cost and inference fidelity.
2606.22510.paper_summary_zh: 雖然聯邦學習使得在去中心化數據上進行協作建模成為可能，但標準方法僅僅擬合歷史觀察。這種純粹的觀察性方法在進行干預推斷和政策評估時根本不夠充分，因為連續的行動會動態改變未來的狀態。我們提出了\textbf{Fed-CausalDiff}，一個用於do-simulation的聯邦因果擴散框架。該架構將潛在狀態的演變分解為一個全局因果分數函數和一個局部混淆分數函數。這一設計實現了\emph{解耦同步}（DSS），客戶端僅聚合共享的因果機制，同時在本地保留特定於站點的混淆因素以處理異質性。在四個數據集上的實驗表明，Fed-CausalDiff在平均處置效應（ATE）和政策價值估計準確性方面表現更佳，提供了通信成本與推斷真實性之間的良好權衡。
2606.22510.author: Pengfei Li et.al.
